product_updates.title: Meta buys robotics startup to bolster its humanoid AI ambitions
product_updates.content: Meta bought humanoid startup Assured Robot Intelligence to beef up its AI models for robots, the company said.

product_updates.title: Did you know you can’t steal a charity? Don’t worry. Elon Musk will remind you.
product_updates.content: Elon Musk spent the better part of three days on the witness stand this week in his lawsuit against OpenAI, and it’s already getting messy. product_updates.title: Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
product_updates.content: The deals come as the DOD has doubled down on diversifying its exposure to AI vendors in the wake of its controversial dispute with Anthropic over usage terms of its AI models.

product_updates.title: ChatGPT Images 2.0 is a hit in India, but not a big winner elsewhere, yet
product_updates.content: Users in India are embracing ChatGPT Images 2.0 for creative, personal visuals — from avatars to cinematic portraits.

technical_breakthroughs.content: In the first week of the landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk took the stand in a crisp black suit and tie and argued that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and president Greg Brockman had deceived him into bankrolling the company. technical_breakthroughs.title: Ubuntu infrastructure has been down for more than a day
technical_breakthroughs.content: The outage has hampered communication concerning a critical vulnerability that gives root.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
technical_breakthroughs.content: Cybersecurity was already under strain before AI entered the stack. Now, as AI expands the attack surface and adds new complexity, the limits of legacy approaches are becoming harder to ignore. This session from MIT Technology Review’s EmTech AI conference explores why security must be rethought …

technical_breakthroughs.title: Operationalizing AI for Scale and Sovereignty
technical_breakthroughs.content: Companies are taking control of their own data to tailor AI for their needs. The challenge lies in balancing ownership with the safe, trusted flow of high‑quality data needed to power reliable insights. technical_breakthroughs.title: Inexpensive seafloor-hopping submersibles could stoke deep-sea science—and mining
technical_breakthroughs.content: Smack dab between Australia and South America, the US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) research vessel Rainier is currently on a mission to map more than 8,000 square nautical miles of the Pacific seafloor in search of critical mineral deposits. technical_breakthroughs.title: Trump’s mass firing just dealt another blow to American science
technical_breakthroughs.content: This past week delivered another gut punch for science in the US. This time, the target was the National Science Foundation—a federal agency that funds major research projects to the tune of around $9 billion. The foundation’s efforts were overseen by a board of 22 prominent scientists. On Friday…

technical_breakthroughs.title: A new US phone network for Christians aims to block porn and gender-related content
technical_breakthroughs.content: A new US-wide cell phone network marketed to Christians is set to launch next week. It blocks porn, which experts in network security say marks the first time a US cell plan has used network-level blocking for such content that can’t be turned off even by adult account owners.
